serving.natural_language_portion: One Spring'O curry puff weighs approximately 65 g and constitutes a single snack serving as sold at Old Chang Kee outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Curry Puff (Karipap) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists a standard Singaporean curry puff (karipap) with chicken-potato filling and fried pastry crust. Spring'O is Old Chang Kee's proprietary spiral-pastry variant of the same product category. Values are adapted from the HPB curry puff entry; the spiral pastry crust is slightly richer in fat than a standard crimped crust, so fat and energy values are adjusted upward by approximately 10% based on the known higher oil absorption of layered spiral pastry. All other macros are held at the HPB reference values.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of curry puff and analogous filled fried pastry — University of Sydney GI Database reference for wheat-flour fried pastry with potato-meat filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Spring'O or Old Chang Kee curry puff. The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values of approximately 50–60 for wheat-flour fried pastry products with starchy fillings (potato-dominant). A GI of 55 is used as the central analogous estimate for a fried wheat-flour pastry with curried potato-chicken filling, consistent with the low GI range for similar products. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 19.8) / 100 ≈ 10.8, where carbohydrate per 65 g serving = 30.5 × 0.65 = 19.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~208 mg per serving) and saturated fat from deep-frying
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Spring'O curry puff to occasional consumption, as one piece contributes approximately 208 mg sodium (about 9% of the 2,300 mg daily limit) and significant saturated fat from the deep-fried spiral pastry crust.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) and refined wheat-flour pastry crust contributing rapidly available carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Spring'O curry puff in moderation; the low GI of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of ~10.8 per piece mean that blood glucose response is moderate, but portion control is important, particularly when combined with other carbohydrate-containing foods in the same meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.7 g per serving) and trans fat from deep-frying in partially hydrogenated or repeatedly used oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit intake of Spring'O curry puff due to its saturated fat content (~4.7 g per piece) and potential trans fat from deep-frying, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spring'O is a signature spiral-pastry curry puff sold by Old Chang Kee, one of Singapore's most iconic snack chains. Unlike the standard crimped-edge curry puff (karipap), the Spring'O features a distinctive hand-rolled spiral or coiled pastry crust that creates multiple flaky, layered rings visible on the exterior. The crust is made from wheat flour dough that is laminated or folded to produce the spiral effect, then deep-fried until golden and crisp. The filling consists of curried potato and chicken seasoned with a blend of curry spices, onion, and aromatics typical of Singaporean-Malay culinary tradition. The result is a snack that is simultaneously crispy on the outside, with a soft, mildly spiced savoury filling inside. Spring'O is sold at approximately SGD 2.30 per piece and is widely available at Old Chang Kee outlets across Singapore, including hawker centres, MRT stations, and shopping malls. Nutritionally, one piece (approximately 65 g) provides around 202 kcal, 4.4 g protein, 12 g fat, and 19.8 g carbohydrate. The low glycemic index (~55) reflects the refined wheat-flour pastry and starchy potato filling. The sodium content (~208 mg per piece) and saturated fat (~4.7 g per piece) are clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The product is halal-certified and dairy-free, making it suitable for Muslim consumers and those avoiding dairy. It is not suitable for individuals with gluten intolerance or wheat allergy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Spring'O curry puff?
What is the difference between Spring'O and a regular curry puff?

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient entry for Spring'O or Old Chang Kee curry puff exists in HPB Foodo or any other approved primary source. Values are adapted from the HPB Singapore standard curry puff (karipap) entry with a modest upward fat adjustment to account for the spiral pastry crust's higher oil absorption. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar fried wheat-flour pastry products. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
